back_translation: The whole earth used the same language and the same words.
When they journeyed from the east, they found a plain in the land of Shinar and settled there.
They said to one another, "Come, let us make bricks and bake them." They used bricks instead of stones, and tar instead of mortar.
They said, "Come, let us build a city and a tower with its top reaching to the sky for ourselves, and let us make a name for ourselves, lest we be scattered over the face of all the earth."
The LORD came down to see the city and the tower that the sons of mankind were building.
The LORD said, "Behold, they are one people, and they have one language; this is just the beginning of what they will do. Now, nothing they intend to do will be impossible for them.
Come, let us go down and confuse their language, so they will not understand each other's words."
The LORD scattered them from there over the face of all the earth, and they stopped building the city.
Therefore, that place was called Babel, because the LORD confused the language of the world there, and from there the LORD scattered them over the face of all the earth.
This is the account of the line of Shem. Two years after the flood, when Shem was 100 years old, he had Arphaxad.
After Arphaxad was born, Shem lived 500 more years and had other sons and daughters.
When Arphaxad was 35 years old, he had Shelah.
After Shelah was born, Arphaxad lived 403 more years and had other sons and daughters.
When Shelah was 30 years old, he had Eber.
After Eber was born, Shelah lived 403 more years and had other sons and daughters.
When Eber was 34 years old, he had Peleg.
After Peleg was born, Eber lived 430 more years and had other sons and daughters.
When Peleg was 30 years old, he had Reu.
After Reu was born, Peleg lived 209 more years and had other sons and daughters.
When Reu was 32 years old, he had Serug.
After Serug was born, Reu lived 207 more years and had other sons and daughters.
When Serug was 30 years old, he had Nahor.
After Nahor was born, Serug lived 200 more years and had other sons and daughters.
When Nahor was 29 years old, he had Terah.
After Terah was born, Nahor lived 119 more years and had other sons and daughters.
When Terah was 70 years old, he had Abram, Nahor, and Haran.
This is the account of the line of Terah. Terah had Abram, Nahor, and Haran. Haran was the father of Lot.
Haran died while his father Terah was still living, in the land of his birth, in Ur of the Chaldeans.
Abram and Nahor each took wives. Abram's wife was named Sarai, and Nahor's wife was named Milcah, the daughter of Haran, who was the father of both Milcah and Iscah.
Sarai was barren; she had no children.

Terah took his son Abram, his grandson Lot (Haran's son), and his daughter-in-law Sarai (Abram's wife), and they set out from Ur of the Chaldeans toward the land of Canaan. But when they arrived at the city of Haran, they settled there.
Terah lived 205 years, and he died in the city of Haran.
